dimension_coverage.evidence_of_check: Read bridge/__init__.py L1-50, compared all public method signatures against spec.md §2.1-2.3
Read bridge/__init__.py L23-L45, bridge/worker.py L10-L80, checked against ADR-001 §3.1
Read all bridge/ files, checked against spec.md §9 corrective items list
Read tests/test_bridge.py, identified assertion tautologies at L45-L48
Ran grep for forbidden imports, AST analysis of transitive dependencies

findings.claim: bridge/__init__.py imports from factor_miner.memory, violating ADR-001 dependency graph invariant (bridge must not depend on memory)

reasoning_chain.checked: DAG acyclicity: bridge must not import from memory
reasoning_chain.found: L23 `from factor_miner.memory import X` violates ADR-001 §3.1
reasoning_chain.confidence_rationale: grep + AST both confirm the import exists and is not TYPE_CHECKING-guarded

findings.fix_suggestion: Use TYPE_CHECKING guard or restructure to remove the direct dependency

findings.claim: Spec corrective item 9.1 (first-mover bias protection) is not implemented — cutoff threshold check is missing from worker.evaluate()

reasoning_chain.checked: Does worker.evaluate() apply a cutoff threshold per spec §9.1?
reasoning_chain.found: No cutoff threshold logic present in evaluate(). Spec §9.1 requires `if delta < cutoff: return None`
reasoning_chain.confidence_rationale: grep confirmed no 'cutoff' keyword in bridge/worker.py

findings.claim: Test assertion at L45-48 is a tautology (`assert result or not result`), providing no actual coverage of bridge evaluation outcomes

dimension_coverage.evidence_of_check: Read memory/*.py, all public methods match spec.md §3.1-3.5
Read memory/store.py L10-L90, memory/query.py L5-L40, checked invariants
Read all memory/ files, spec §9.3-9.5 corrective items all confirmed implemented
Read tests/test_memory.py, found no test for schema_version field
grep confirmed only orchestrator calls memory.write_*()

findings.claim: No test for schema_version field in P_succRecord, though spec §9.10 mandates version field for future compatibility

dimension_coverage.evidence_of_check: Checked all ADR invariants, dependency graphs, unique entry points

findings.claim: orchestrator/main.py L88 calls registry.get() directly instead of through DSL interpreter, violating ADR-001 unique entry point invariant

reasoning_chain.checked: Only DSL interpreter may call registry.get()
reasoning_chain.found: L88 `result = registry.get(op_name)` is a direct call bypassing DSL
reasoning_chain.confidence_rationale: grep confirmed no DSL wrapper around this call

findings.fix_suggestion: Route through DSL interpreter: `result = dsl.evaluate(ast_node)` instead of direct registry access

skipped_dimensions.reason: Cross-module contract audit focuses on invariants, not per-module behavior
Cross-module contract audit focuses on invariants, not per-module corrective items
Cross-module contract audit focuses on invariants, not per-module test coverage
